 {"id":"bd-1jn","title":"Impl: Runtime hardening (limits, watchdog, accounting)","description":"# Goal

 {"id":"bd-1mh","title":"Update session picker to use SQLite index","description":"# Update session picker to use SQLite index

## Goal
Replace filesystem walking in session picker with fast SQLite index queries.

## Current Implementation (src/session_picker.rs)
Currently scans filesystem:
```rust
// Slow: walks all session directories
let sessions = walkdir::WalkDir::new(&sessions_dir)
    .into_iter()
    .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
    .filter(|e| e.path().extension() == Some("jsonl"))
    .collect();
```

## New Implementation
```rust
use crate::session_index::SessionIndex;

impl SessionPicker {
    pub fn load_sessions(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
        let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
        index.list_sessions(cwd)
    }
}
```

## UI Changes
- Display indexed metadata directly (no file reads needed)
- Show message count from index
- Show session name from index
- Faster render (no filesystem I/O)

## Fallback Behavior
If index unavailable or empty:
```rust
fn load_sessions_with_fallback(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
    // Try index first
    if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
        if let Ok(sessions) = index.list_sessions(cwd) {
            if !sessions.is_empty() {
                return Ok(sessions);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem (and populate index)
    let sessions = load_sessions_from_filesystem(cwd)?;
    // Optionally trigger reindex in background
    Ok(sessions)
}
```

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: Session picker shows indexed sessions
- Test: Picker falls back to filesystem if needed
- Test: Performance improved (measure time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker queries index by default
- [ ] Fallback to filesystem works
- [ ] UI displays index metadata
- [ ] Noticeably faster for 50+ sessions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.861448590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:59.101819926Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.870643664Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-3nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:59.101795250Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1pf","title":"Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming","description":"# Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming

## Goal
Create infrastructure to record and replay real API responses for provider testing.

## Background
VCR (Video Cassette Recorder) testing pattern:
1. First run: Make real API call, record response to file
2. Subsequent runs: Replay recorded response, no network

This gives us:
- Real response format testing (not simplified mocks)
- Deterministic CI (no flaky network tests)
- Real error response testing (rate limits, auth failures)

## Implementation

### Recording Mode
```rust
// tests/vcr/mod.rs

pub struct VcrRecorder {
    cassette_path: PathBuf,
    mode: VcrMode,
}

pub enum VcrMode {
    Record,    // Make real calls, save to cassette
    Playback,  // Replay from cassette, fail if not found
    Auto,      // Playback if exists, else record
}

impl VcrRecorder {
    pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> Self { ... }

    pub async fn request(&self, req: Request) -> Response {
        match self.mode {
            VcrMode::Record => self.record_and_return(req).await,
            VcrMode::Playback => self.playback(req),
            VcrMode::Auto => ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Cassette Format
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "test_name": "anthropic_streaming_text",
  "recorded_at": "2026-02-02T...",
  "interactions": [
    {
      "request": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        "headers": { ... },
        "body": { ... }
      },
      "response": {
        "status": 200,
        "headers": { ... },
        "body_chunks": [
          "event: message_start\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          "event: content_block_delta\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          ...
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### SSE Streaming Support
For streaming responses, store individual chunks:
- Each SSE event as separate chunk
- Preserve timing information (optional replay delay)
- Support partial responses (stream interruption)

### Sensitive Data Redaction
```rust
pub fn redact_cassette(cassette: &mut Cassette) {
    // Replace API keys with placeholder
    cassette.redact_header("x-api-key", "[REDACTED]");
    cassette.redact_header("authorization", "[REDACTED]");

    // Replace sensitive response data
    cassette.redact_body_field("api_key", "[REDACTED]");
}
```

## Goal
Achieve 95%+ test coverage using real implementations wherever possible, with
detailed logging for E2E integration tests. Eliminate reliance on mocks for
core functionality testing.

## Background
Current test coverage analysis reveals critical gaps:

### Well Tested (Good)
- Built-in Tools: 100% coverage (122 conformance fixture cases)
- Session Tree Logic: 11 tests covering branching/navigation
- SSE Parser: 9 tests covering all edge cases
- Auth Flow: 3 tests for PKCE/OAuth setup

### Critical Gaps (Bad)
- **Provider Streaming**: 0 tests with real API/streaming (only unit tests)
- **Interactive TUI**: 2 tests out of 2842 lines (<0.1%)
- **CLI/main.rs**: 0 tests for 1581 lines
- **Compaction**: 0 tests for 903 lines
- **Configuration**: 0 tests for 332 lines
- **Session Persistence**: Only serialization tested, not full I/O cycle

### What "No Mocks" Means
- Provider tests: Use VCR-style recorded responses, not fake providers
- File tests: Use real temp directories (already done)
- CLI tests: Use actual binary execution
- TUI tests: Use terminal emulator or snapshot testing
- Network tests: Use local test server or recorded responses

## Testing Strategy

### 1. Provider Streaming Tests (VCR Style)
Record real API responses and replay them:
- No external network calls during CI
- Real response parsing (not simplified mocks)
- Error scenarios from real API errors

### 2. E2E Integration Tests
Full workflow tests with verbose logging:
- Start pi binary with arguments
- Verify output and exit codes
- Check session files created
- Log every step for debugging

### 3. Snapshot Testing for TUI
Capture terminal output and compare:
- Render TUI components to string
- Compare against golden snapshots
- Update snapshots on intentional changes

### 4. Property-Based Testing
Use proptest for edge cases:
- Truncation with random inputs
- Session serialization round-trips
- Path resolution edge cases

### 5. Compaction Tests
- Window selection + summary insertion
- Tool/thinking/image content handling
- Session invariants preserved after compaction

### 6. Configuration Tests
- Config discovery + precedence rules
- Env + CLI override behavior
- Invalid config errors with actionable messages

- [ ] Provider streaming tested with recorded responses

- [ ] CLI argument parsing fully tested

 {"id":"bd-3d8","title":"Implement /theme slash command","description":"# Implement /theme slash command

## Goal
Add /theme command to list available themes and switch between them.

## Command Syntax
```
/theme              - List available themes
/theme dark         - Switch to dark theme
/theme ocean-dark   - Switch to custom theme
```

## Implementation

### Command Handler
```rust
// src/interactive.rs

fn handle_theme_command(&mut self, args: &str) -> Cmd {
    if args.is_empty() {
        // List themes
        let themes = Theme::discover_themes();
        let current = &self.theme.name;
        
        let mut output = String::from("Available themes:\n");
        for path in themes {
            if let Ok(theme) = Theme::load(&path) {
                let marker = if theme.name == *current { "* " } else { "  " };
                output.push_str(&format!("{}{}\n", marker, theme.name));
            }
        }
        output.push_str("\nUse /theme <name> to switch");
        
        self.status_message = Some(output);
        Cmd::None
    } else {
        // Switch theme
        match Theme::load_by_name(args.trim()) {
            Ok(theme) => {
                self.theme = theme.clone();
                self.styles = create_styles(&theme);
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Switched to theme: {}", theme.name));
                
                // Persist preference
                if let Err(e) = self.save_theme_preference(&theme.name) {
                    tracing::warn!("Failed to save theme preference: {}", e);
                }
                
                Cmd::None
            }
            Err(_) => {
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Theme not found: {}", args.trim()));
                Cmd::None
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Persist Theme Preference
```rust
fn save_theme_preference(&self, theme_name: &str) -> Result<()> {
    // Update settings.json with theme preference
    let settings_path = Config::settings_path();
    let mut settings = Config::load_from_file(&settings_path)?;
    settings.theme = Some(theme_name.to_string());
    settings.save_to_file(&settings_path)?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### Add to Slash Command Router
```rust
fn handle_slash_command(&mut self, input: &str) -> Cmd {
    let (cmd, args) = parse_slash_command(input);
    
    match cmd {
        "theme" => self.handle_theme_command(args),
        "help" => self.handle_help_command(),
        // ... other commands
    }
}
```

### Update /help Output
```
Commands:
  /help      - Show this help
  /model     - Switch model
  /thinking  - Set thinking level
  /theme     - List/switch themes  <-- NEW
  /clear     - Clear conversation
  /exit      - Exit Pi
```

 {"id":"bd-hqq","title":"Unit tests: compaction pipeline + summary invariants","description":"# Unit tests: compaction pipeline + summary invariants

## Goal
Add comprehensive tests for compaction logic to ensure summaries are correct,
message windows are respected, and session invariants hold — without mocks.

## Scope
- Compaction window selection (token budget, message count, system prompt rules).
- Summary insertion semantics (where summary is injected, ordering preserved).
- Handling of tool calls, thinking blocks, and image/content blocks.
- Edge cases: tiny budgets, already-compacted sessions, empty assistant response.
- Session integrity: parent/child IDs, current leaf, and compaction entry metadata.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log input message count, token budget, selected span indices, and summary length.
- On failure, dump the compacted timeline (message IDs + types + brief text).

## Determinism Requirements
- Use fixed fixtures for token counts and message content (no randomness).
- No network usage; temp dirs only if persistence is exercised.

## Files
- tests/compaction.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ compaction tests
- [ ] Window selection respects budget and role ordering
- [ ] Summary entry inserted correctly
- [ ] Tool + thinking blocks preserved or summarized as specified
- [ ] Session tree invariants preserved
- [ ] Logs include compaction selection + summary metadata","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:29.310486765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T09:16:21.984938067Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T09:16:21.984876392Z","close_reason":"Implemented compaction tests + invariants; gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hqq","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:29.317387163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hqq","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:45:34.801055578Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
